description: Capability set for the dedicated Messages window (US-009). The shell self-fetches its data via app-registered Tauri commands authorized by core:default (custom commands are not gated by per-command permission identifiers in this ACL — only plugins are), so no allow-* tokens are added for them. The window invokes: messages_window_ready (ready-handshake show/focus), open_messages_window (focus from within), list_contacts, list_company_members, get_unread_summary, plus the shared messaging commands fetch_dm_thread + send_dm (the conversation pane reuses the DM thread + composer) and send_dm_to_email (US-010 New Message compose — POSTs /v1/notify/dm by email or personUid and returns the delivered vs connection-requested discriminant), and the connection-request commands list_dm_requests + respond_dm_request (US-011 Requests segment — list_dm_requests reads GET /v1/notify/connections/requests for the pending-request cards, respond_dm_request POSTs /v1/notify/connections/{accept|decline|block} with {pairKey} and emits dm:request-update on success). US-018 Channels segment adds the channel commands list_channels (GET /v1/notify/channels), fetch_channel (GET /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages), create_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels), join_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members, self), invite_to_channel (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members, {personUids}), send_channel_message (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages), list_channel_members (GET /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members), remove_channel_member (DELETE /v1/notify/channels/{id}/members/{personUid}), and mark_channel_read (POST /v1/notify/channels/{id}/read); the channel realtime events channel:new-message + channel:updated are emitted by the SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. US-022 Threads UI adds the thread commands fetch_thread (GET /v1/notify/threads?rootEventId=&scope=dm|channel[&channelId=|&withPersonUid=] — pinned root + reply list + replyCount for the right-side ThreadPanel), send_thread_reply (POST /v1/notify/dm or /v1/notify/channels/{id}/messages with an added rootEventId to post a reply), and set_active_thread (registers/clears which thread the ThreadPanel currently has open so the SINGLE poll path knows which thread to re-fetch on a 'thread' wake); the thread realtime event thread:new-reply is emitted by that SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. US-025 Reactions UI adds the reaction commands toggle_reaction (POST add / DELETE remove /v1/notify/reactions with {messageScope, messageId, emoji}) and fetch_reactions (GET /v1/notify/reactions?messageScope=&messageId= → [{emoji, count, reactedByMe}]), plus set_active_conversation (registers/clears which conversation + visible message ids the open Conversation pane shows, so the SINGLE poll path knows which messages to re-fetch reactions for on a 'reaction' wake); the reaction realtime event message:reaction is emitted by that SINGLE DM poll path and authorized by core:event:default. The compose recipient picker (RecipientPicker.svelte) additionally invokes meetings_list_memberships (to enumerate the caller's companies for per-company member groups). All HTTP to /v1/notify/* happens in Rust, not the webview, so no http: scope is needed here. Capability drift (a command registered in main.rs but missing from this window's capability file) is the #1 failure mode — every command callable from this window must remain listed in the description above and authorized by core:default below. Mirrors desktop-alt.json / dm-detail.json. Share integration: open_messages_window now accepts an optional deep-link target (stashed in PendingMessagesTarget and delivered via the messages:open-conversation event on ready/focus); the shell additionally invokes set_watched_shares + toggle_reaction/fetch_reactions with share:{eventId} scopes for the inline share-card bubbles merged into DM threads, fetch_notification_history (shares feed the timeline + rail previews), and open_claude_code_link (share card 'Open in Claude').
